title: For Trump, the Iran War Looms Over China Summit
summary: The balance of power between the United States and China had shifted in Beijing’s favor even before the war in Iran began in February.

title: Chinese Firms Plot Secret Arms Sales to Iran, U.S. Officials Say
summary: The effort involves plans to send weapons through other countries in an effort to hide the origins of the shipments.

title: Why the Bombing of Iran Tied the U.S. More Closely to China
summary: As the U.S. tries to rebuild its weapons stockpiles drained in the Iran war, it will need access to rare-earth minerals, an industry China dominates.

summary: The relationship between the two leaders is marked just as much by mistrust and confrontation as it is by niceties.

summary: Beijing welcomed President Trump with a high-ranking vice president, but the choice of a ceremonial leader suggests China is trading symbolism for substance.

summary: The decision, in which judges cited jury interference by a court clerk, upends one of America’s highest-profile homicide cases.

title: Walking the Streets of Ben Franklin’s Paris
summary: The aging statesman braved rough seas and arduous carriage rides to reach Paris, where he persuaded the French to back the American rebels. We followed in his footsteps.

title: Why U.S. Test Scores Are in a ‘Generation-Long Decline’
summary: The drops go beyond the pandemic and cut across income, geographic and racial divides, new data shows.

summary: Senator Lisa Murkowski of Alaska became the latest Republican to switch her vote to halt the conflict and require President Trump to win congressional approval to continue it.

title: With a Friend in Trump, the Tobacco Industry Secures a Lucrative Win
summary: In a dispute over vapes, the president sided with tobacco companies that filled his groups’ coffers over his own F.D.A. commissioner, who resigned in protest.

title: Rich Danker, RFK Jr.’s Top Spokesman, Resigns in Protest Over New Vaping Policy
summary: In a letter to President Trump, the spokesman, Rich Danker, said allowing the sale of flavored e-cigarettes would enhance their appeal to children.

title: Tariff Refunds Begin to Reach Businesses as Trump Lashes Out at Court
summary: The government must return about $160 billion, plus interest, collected from duties deemed illegal and potentially more if it loses a related tariff case.

title: An Uncertain World Awaits the Reopening of the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Fatih Birol of the International Energy Agency, who orchestrated a multinational release of oil reserves, detailed the risks facing the economy now and beyond.

summary: Electric vehicle sales have soared in Europe and much of the rest of the world, but Americans are still hesitant.

title: Wholesale Prices Jumped in April, in Latest Sign of War’s Economic Ripples
summary: The Producer Price Index rose in April at its fastest pace in four years, government data showed, a day after consumer prices showed inflation was surging.

title: Billionaire Rick Jackson Is Upending the Georgia Governor’s Race
summary: Rick Jackson was largely unknown to voters just a few months ago. Heading into Tuesday’s Republican primary, he’s keeping some of the state’s best-known politicians on their toes.

title: Andreessen Horowitz Is Playing Politics Like No Other
summary: “If you think there’s a lot of money in politics now,” Marc Andreessen said in 2000, “you haven’t seen anything yet.” His firm is now the biggest known spender on this campaign cycle.

title: Trump Administration Pushes I.R.S. to Identify Undocumented Immigrants
summary: The deliberations are a sign that the Trump administration may once again try to harness the power of the I.R.S. to advance its immigration agenda.

title: ICE Detains Ex-Kansas Mayor Joe Ceballos Who Voted Illegally
summary: Joe Ceballos said he did not know he was ineligible to vote and run for office as a green-card holder. The Trump administration has drawn attention to the case.

title: Eating Healthy? No, They’re Eating Biblically.
summary: A diet inspired by the Bible has found new audiences online in the Make America Healthy Again era.

summary: The woman, who was a passenger on the MV Hondius, was breathing with the help of an artificial lung, officials in Paris said.

summary: Here’s what to know about the virus, how it spreads and the risk to the general public.

title: Addiction Counselor Who Helped Supply Ketamine to Matthew Perry Sentenced to Two Years
summary: Erik Fleming, a licensed drug addiction counselor, had admitted to selling 51 vials of ketamine to the actor, including the dose that killed him.

title: Dozens of Polymarket Bets Show Signs of Insider Trading, The Times Finds
summary: Dozens of long-shot bets on Polymarket, from the war with Iran to the cryptocurrency market, have defied the odds, according to a New York Times examination.

title: Trump’s China Policy Has Weakened America
summary: In Beijing this week, Mr. Trump should not hand China more victories.

title: We Cannot Let Trump Cut Childhood Cancer Research
summary: More children will die because the Trump administration is cutting pediatric cancer research.

title: Corpus Christi Faces Water Crisis as Drought and Industrial Growth Strain Supply
summary: Rising demand, municipal dysfunction and drought have pushed Corpus Christi to the edge of a water emergency, offering a cautionary tale for the rest of the country.

title: Chaos in Philippines Senate as Duterte Ally Faces Arrest
summary: The sound of gunshots, apparently from inside the chamber, was broadcast on live television as a senator in the Philippines, who was an ally of the former leader Rodrigo Duterte, faced arrest.

title: N.Y.U. Students Object to Graduation Speaker Jonathan Haidt, Who Calls Their Generation ‘Coddled’
summary: Jonathan Haidt, a professor, says that colleges shield students from challenging ideas. But student leaders said he does not represent their values.

title: Are These the Bones of the Fourth Musketeer? This Dutch Village Hopes So.
summary: Wolder, near the Belgian border, is waiting to see if the skeleton it dug up in a church is Count d’Artagnan, from Alexandre Dumas’s tale.
